reason: Bitcoin experienced a short-lived surge to $74,000, but buyers are quickly cashing out, indicating strong selling pressure and potential distribution. This is exacerbated by negative macroeconomic data (unexpected job losses in the U.S.), heightened geopolitical tensions (Trump's stance on Iran sending risk assets lower), and a broad downturn across the CoinDesk 20 index. While Binance denied direct crypto transfers to Iran, regulatory scrutiny remains a background risk. The confluence of these factors points to a high-risk environment with significant downside potential.
headlines: Bitcoin buyers are cashing out fast after short-lived jump to $74,000
Binance tells Senate probe no accounts sent crypto directly to Iran
'No deal with Iran': Trump demands unconditional surrender, sending oil surging, bitcoin and stocks lower
CoinDesk 20 performance update: Aave drops 4.3% as all index constituents trade lower
U.S. unexpectedly lost 92,000 jobs in February, unemployment rate rose to 4.4%
